_meta.provenance: Hand-curated from public sources (Wikidata, SEC EDGAR, company filings & investor materials), 2025-2026. Not Diffbot-derived; the curated compilation is licensed CC-BY-4.0.
_meta.description: MarketMind macro/exogenous layer. Seed-only — NOT traversed by the cascade.

commodities.name: Neon (semiconductor-grade)
commodities.geoConcentration: Ukraine ~70% of global semiconductor-grade supply (Ingas/Cryoin, Odesa & Mariupol); purified as a steelmaking byproduct, much feedstock historically from Russia/China. China is the other large producer.

commodities.name: Wholesale electricity / power
commodities.geoConcentration: Regional/grid-bound (not globally traded); US merchant power concentrated in PJM/ERCOT. Datacenter-driven demand growth is the swing factor.

commodities.name: Crude oil
commodities.geoConcentration: Globally traded (Brent/WTI); supply concentrated in OPEC+ (~40% of output), US shale, and Russia.

commodities.name: Lithium
commodities.geoConcentration: Mining: Australia ~50%, Chile + Argentina (lithium triangle) ~25%; refining/processing China ~65-70%.

commodities.name: Rare earth elements (NdPr / magnet)
commodities.geoConcentration: China ~70% of mining and ~85-90% of refining/separation; MP Materials (Mountain Pass) is the main ex-China mine (~15% of global content).

themes.name: AI Datacenter Capex
themes.description: The hyperscaler/sovereign AI infrastructure build-out thesis. Names move on the expectation of sustained, rising spend on AI accelerators, custom silicon, networking and full racks. This is the basket that DeepSeek (2025-01-27) re-rated: a cheap-compute shock that questions the capex trajectory hits every member, even those with no direct DeepSeek linkage. Sign +1 means the stock rises when the AI-capex narrative strengthens.

themes.name: High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Cycle
themes.description: The AI-memory super-cycle: HBM (stacked DRAM for AI accelerators) plus the back-end packaging tools (TC bonders, hybrid bonding) that gate HBM supply. A sub-narrative of AI-capex but with its own pricing/supply dynamics (HBM is sold out, ASP-rich, and capacity-constrained), so members can decouple from broad logic. Sign +1: stock rises when HBM demand/pricing tightens.

themes.name: AI Power Demand
themes.description: The 'electricity is the bottleneck for AI' thesis: independent power producers (esp. nuclear), grid/transmission build-out, and datacenter electrical/cooling/backup gear all re-rate on expectations that AI datacenters drive a step-change in US power demand. Sign +1: stock rises as the power-demand narrative strengthens. BEAR CASE: this theme is structurally DOWNSTREAM of AI-capex — if AI capex disappoints (a DeepSeek-style efficiency shock, or a hyperscaler capex cut), the power-demand thesis deflates too, so members also carry positive AI-capex loadings and can sell off in an AI de-rating despite no fundamental change in current power load.

themes.name: US-China Semiconductor Export Controls
themes.description: Policy-risk factor for the US/allied chip & equipment complex exposed to China-revenue restrictions vs. the Chinese domestic champions that benefit from forced import-substitution. A TIGHTENING of controls is the shock: Western accelerator and WFE (wafer-fab-equipment) makers lose addressable China demand (sign -1), while protected, state-backed Chinese foundries gain share and subsidy (sign +1). Loadings reflect China-revenue / China-orderbook transmission, not operational dependency.

themes.name: China Tech / ADR Basket
themes.description: The China internet + domestic-chip basket beta: US-listed ADRs and HK-listed China-tech names that move together on China macro/regulatory risk-on/risk-off (stimulus, regulatory thaw, delisting/geopolitical headlines). Sign +1: stock rises on a China-tech re-rating (e.g. the Sep-2024 PBOC stimulus rally). Captures common basket factor exposure that no company-to-company edge expresses.

themes.name: EV Transition
themes.description: The global shift to battery-electric vehicles and its battery/materials supply chain. Pure-play EV makers, battery cell manufacturers and lithium producers re-rate on EV demand/penetration expectations, price wars, and subsidy/policy shifts. Sign +1: stock rises as EV-transition momentum strengthens. Note the embedded tension for legacy/diversified OEMs, where an aggressive EV transition is volume-positive but margin-dilutive (lower loading).

themes.name: AI Drug Discovery / TechBio
themes.description: The 'AI applied to biology' thesis: computational drug discovery, AI-driven diagnostics/genomics, and platform TechBio names. A small, distinct sub-narrative of the broader AI trade — these stocks catch sentiment from AI-capex risk-on/risk-off but are NOT levered to hyperscaler accelerator spend, so they get a modest AI-capex loading plus a primary loading here rather than fake NVDA-supply edges. Sign +1: rises on AI-in-biology enthusiasm.

conditions.description: US Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) advanced-computing & semiconductor-manufacturing export controls on China, enacted 2022-10-07. Bans export of leading-edge AI accelerators (A100/H100 class) and advanced wafer-fab equipment (EUV/advanced DUV, advanced etch/deposition/metrology) to Chinese fabs; restricts US-person support. Tightened 2023-10-17 (A800/H800/L40 loophole closure) and again Dec-2024. Hurts US/EU chip and WFE exporters that lose China revenue; helps Chinese domestic foundries (SMIC, Hua Hong) via forced localization / self-sufficiency demand.

US Section 301 tariffs on Chinese imports, first effective 2018-07-06 (List 1, 25%) and progressively broadened; still active. Hurts US firms with China-manufactured hardware or large China import/revenue exposure (Apple supply chain, autos, EV-component imports) and Chinese exporters into the US; a renewed/expanded China-tariff stance under the 2024 Trump win is the policy overhang re-priced by markets.

exposureEdges.source: ExxonMobil is an integrated oil major; upstream earnings track crude prices, so XOM moves strongly same-sign with crude.
MP Materials is a near-pure-play NdPr/rare-earth producer (Mountain Pass); its equity is highly levered to rare-earth (esp. NdPr) prices.
FCX is primarily a copper/gold producer (no commodity in this lite set); only a weak, indirect critical-minerals readthrough. Low weight, flagged as soft.

Linde is a top global industrial-gas major that purifies/sells semiconductor-grade neon, but neon is a tiny fraction of revenue; transmission to LIN stock is small (mostly a pricing-power tailwind during shortages).
Air Liquide is the other major industrial-gas producer of rare gases incl. neon; same as Linde, neon is immaterial to group revenue, so weak same-sign transmission.
Neon is a key DUV excimer-laser lithography gas; a neon spike/shortage is an input-cost and supply-continuity risk for the leading-edge fab. War->neon->chips chain. Opposite-sign (cost up = bad).
Large memory+logic fab; significant DUV-litho neon consumer. Input-cost/continuity risk on a neon shock.
IDM with large DUV-litho footprint; neon-shortage exposure on the cost/continuity side.
DRAM/NAND fabs run heavy DUV lithography; neon-supply shock is an operational/cost risk.
SK hynix memory fabs are major DUV-litho neon consumers; opposite-sign cost/continuity exposure.
Constellation is the largest US nuclear/merchant-power generator; earnings and stock are highly levered to wholesale power prices (and datacenter PPA demand). Same-sign.
Vistra is a merchant power generator (gas/nuclear/solar); equity is a high-beta play on power prices and AI-datacenter demand. Same-sign.
NRG is an integrated power/retail generator; levered to power prices but more hedged/retail-buffered than pure merchants, so somewhat lower transmission.
Datacenter power is a real and growing cost, but immaterial to MSFT margins today; weak opposite-sign cost transmission (power->datacenter chain). Low weight, honest.
Heavy AI-datacenter power consumer; power cost is a growing but still small share of opex. Weak opposite-sign transmission.
Largest hyperscale datacenter operator; rising power cost is a margin headwind but small vs total. Non-tradable node (propagates to AMZN). Weak opposite-sign.
Hyperscale datacenter power consumer; weak opposite-sign cost transmission. Non-tradable node (propagates to GOOGL).
Fabs are extremely power-intensive (TSMC is a top single consumer of Taiwan's electricity); power cost/availability is a real but margin-buffered headwind. Weak-to-moderate opposite-sign.
High oil/gasoline prices dampen demand for ICE/large trucks (Ford's profit pool) and raise input/logistics costs; opposite-sign demand transmission.
High oil hits demand for GM's ICE truck/SUV mix and raises costs; opposite-sign demand transmission.
Toyota is more hybrid-weighted, so less oil-sensitive than US truck makers, but high oil still pressures volumes/costs. Opposite-sign, smaller.

SUBSTITUTION angle: high crude/gasoline prices IMPROVE the relative economics of EVs, a demand tailwind for Tesla. Modeled as CONSUMES with POSITIVE sign (sign-flipped vs a normal consumer) to capture the oil->EV substitution. Explicitly noted.
Same EV substitution angle: high oil aids EV/PHEV adoption (BYD also sells PHEVs, so slightly weaker pure-EV effect). CONSUMES with POSITIVE sign. Noted.
Albemarle is the largest pure-play lithium producer; equity tracks lithium (spodumene/carbonate) prices very closely. Strong same-sign.
World's largest battery maker; lithium is a major cell input cost. Note: CATL passes through cost and benefits from volume, so net stock effect is partly offset, hence moderate opposite-sign weight.
Major battery maker; lithium input-cost exposure, partly passed through. Moderate opposite-sign.
Battery maker (Tesla supplier) but lithium is a smaller share of diversified Panasonic; opposite-sign, smaller.
Lithium is a battery input cost for Tesla's vehicles/energy storage; high lithium = margin headwind. Opposite-sign, modest (Tesla buys cells/has supply deals).
BYD is vertically integrated (makes its own batteries), so lithium price is a direct input cost. Opposite-sign.
NdFeB permanent magnets are used in EV traction motors; a rare-earth squeeze (esp. China export curbs) is a cost/continuity risk. Small share of cost, weak transmission.
EV motor magnets use rare earths (NdPr); rare-earth supply shock is an input/continuity risk. Weak opposite-sign.
Permanent-magnet motors use rare earths; supply/price shock is a cost/continuity risk (Tesla has moved to cut rare-earth use, so modest). Opposite-sign.
EV motor magnets; rare-earth input/continuity exposure (China-sourced, so closer to supply). Opposite-sign.

exposureEdges.channel: Bellwether of the AI-accelerator capex trade; the stock IS the AI-capex narrative (record $589B 1-day loss on the DeepSeek capex-doubt shock).
Custom AI silicon (XPUs) + AI networking for hyperscalers; guided $60-90B AI TAM. Moves tightly with the capex thesis (-17% on DeepSeek, +24% on its own AI guide).
Custom AI ASIC / AI-datacenter optics/networking; highest-torque small-cap proxy for hyperscaler silicon spend.
Sole leading-edge + CoWoS manufacturer for AI accelerators; AI HPC is the dominant revenue-growth driver, but diversified (mobile/auto) caps the loading below the pure-plays.
Highest-beta AI server/rack integrator; revenue is almost entirely AI-datacenter systems. (Idiosyncratic accounting overhang adds non-theme volatility.)
AI server backlog is the growth/re-rating driver, but a large legacy PC/enterprise base dilutes the AI-capex transmission.
AI-datacenter Ethernet networking; revenue concentrated in hyperscalers, so the stock trades on AI back-end-network capex.
MI-series AI accelerators are the swing growth story, but a large non-AI CPU/client/embedded base dilutes the pure capex beta.
IPP with nuclear + gas fleet; lead datacenter-power re-rating name (Talen/Constellation-style PPAs), stock moves on AI power-demand headlines.
Largest US nuclear fleet; signed datacenter PPAs (incl. Microsoft/Three Mile Island restart). The flagship AI-power-demand stock.
Independent power / retail; benefits from the AI load-growth and tight-capacity narrative, but more retail/hedged than the nuclear pure-plays.
Gas turbines + grid equipment; order book re-rates on datacenter-driven generation and grid build-out.
Electrical power-management gear for datacenters; AI datacenter electrification is the key growth/multiple driver, diluted by a broad industrial base.
Datacenter thermal/power (cooling, busbar, UPS); a near-pure AI-datacenter capex+power-density play, high transmission.
Electrical infrastructure / transmission EPC; benefits from grid build-out for AI load growth, diluted by broad utility/telecom work.
BEAR-CASE LINK: power-demand thesis is downstream of AI capex; VST sold off in the DeepSeek capex-doubt shock despite no change to current load.
BEAR-CASE LINK: datacenter-PPA re-rating is contingent on the AI build-out continuing; de-rates if AI capex stalls.
BEAR-CASE LINK: turbine/grid order-book optimism is tied to sustained AI datacenter spend.
BEAR-CASE LINK: datacenter thermal/power demand is a direct function of AI capex; falls with the accelerator trade.
HBM market-share leader and lead supplier to NVIDIA; the purest HBM-cycle stock (record Q3-24 profit on HBM/AI demand).
HBM3E ramp is the re-rating driver, but a large commodity DRAM/NAND base ties it to the broader memory cycle, diluting pure-HBM beta.
HBM upside is real but it is the share-loser/laggard in HBM qualification, and a huge diversified franchise (foundry/handset/display) dilutes transmission.
Dominant TC-bonder supplier for HBM stacking; a near-pure HBM-capex equipment proxy — stock is one of the highest-beta HBM plays.
Hybrid-bonding leader — the next-gen HBM/advanced-packaging tool optionality drives the multiple; partially diluted by mainstream assembly cyclicality.
Loses high-end China accelerator revenue with each tightening (H20/A800/H800 restrictions); sign -1, but China is a minority of revenue so loading is moderate.
EUV already barred; DUV-to-China curbs and a large recent China backlog mean each tightening is a direct demand headwind. Sign -1.
High China WFE revenue share; deposition/etch/CMP tools are squarely in the entity-list/curb scope. Sign -1.
Etch/deposition tools to Chinese fabs heavily curbed; among the most China-WFE-exposed US equipment names. Sign -1.
Process-control/metrology tools to China restricted; meaningful China revenue share. Sign -1.

Sign +1 (winner): controls entrench it as the protected, state-subsidized domestic foundry champion via forced import-substitution; partly offset by being cut off from leading-edge tools, hence moderate loading.
Bellwether US-listed China-internet ADR; the lead basket name in China risk-on/off (jumped on Sep-2024 PBOC stimulus).
Largest HK-listed China-tech weight; core of the China-tech basket beta.
US-listed China internet/AI ADR; moves with the basket on China macro/regulatory sentiment.
US-listed China e-commerce ADR; high basket co-movement on China risk-on/off.
HK-listed China consumer-tech; trades with the China-tech basket plus its own EV/hardware idiosyncrasies (slightly diluted).
HK-listed China domestic-chip champion; rallies with the China-tech / chip-self-sufficiency basket.
Global pure-play EV bellwether; the stock IS the EV-transition narrative (though increasingly priced on AI/robotaxi optionality too, slightly capping loading).
World's top NEV seller; pure-play beneficiary of EV penetration and the EV price war (overtook Tesla on volume).
Chinese pure-play EV startup; high-beta to EV demand/penetration and subsidy sentiment.
Legacy OEM: EV transition is volume-positive but margin-dilutive and a minority of the franchise, so transmission is partial and ambiguous (lower loading).
World's largest EV battery maker; directly levered to global EV battery demand.
Tesla/North-America battery supplier — EV batteries are the growth driver, but a large diversified industrial/appliance base dilutes the EV beta.
Largest lithium producer; share price tracks EV-battery demand/lithium-price expectations (sign +1 on the EV-demand axis, though oversupply can invert the lithium-price leg).
Pure-play AI drug-discovery platform; stock trades on the AI-in-biology thesis and platform/partnership newsflow.
Computational/physics-based drug-design software + pipeline; core AI-drug-discovery name.
AI-driven precision-medicine diagnostics/data; a flagship TechBio/AI-healthcare stock.
Genomics-sequencing incumbent; catches AI-in-biology sentiment but is a mature, fundamentals-driven tools company, so low thematic transmission.
Small AI-sentiment beta only: catches AI risk-on/off, NOT hyperscaler accelerator spend (no fake NVDA-supply edge).
Small AI-sentiment beta only; not levered to datacenter capex.
Small AI-sentiment beta only; 'AI' label drives some co-movement with the broad AI trade, not capex fundamentals.

BIS rule names MI/AI accelerators; AMD flagged China datacenter-GPU sales restricted
EUV banned to China; advanced-DUV (NXT:2000i+) restricted under US+NL controls; China was a large bookings share
BIS WFE controls curb deposition/etch tool exports to advanced Chinese fabs; China a major revenue region
Lam etch/deposition equipment exports to China curbed; large China revenue exposure
KLA process-control/metrology tools restricted; US-person support to Chinese fabs limited
Tokyo Electron WFE exports to China curbed under aligned Japan controls (Jul-2023); large China sales mix

Hua Hong is a mature-node localization beneficiary of forced China self-sufficiency under the controls
China-concentrated assembly + China end-market revenue; Section 301 tariff escalation is a recurring overhang
